billing.purchase_ai_credits.summary.add_.string: As a reminder, your plan renews {renewalDate, date, medium}. You can head to your Billing dashboard to see how updating credits impact annual costs.

billing.purchase_ai_credits.summary.disc.string: By clicking “Confirm,” you agree to the {figmaAiTermsLink}. Your AI credit subscription will be prorated to align with your Figma annual plan and charged on your next invoice. Going forward, it will automatically renew, and you’ll be charged the above amount annually unless you cancel this subscription in the Admin dashboard within the 30-day window before your next renewal.

By clicking “Confirm,” you agree to the {figmaAiTermsLink}. Your AI credit subscription will be prorated to align your Figma Order Term and charged on your next invoice. If your current contract is set to auto-renew, your AI credit subscription will auto-renew and you’ll be charged the above amount annually, unless you cancel this subscription by providing notice to your customer representative or by initiating a support request at {contactSupportLink} at least 30 days before the end of your then-current subscription period.

By clicking “Confirm,” you agree to the {figmaAiTermsLink}. Your AI credit subscription will be prorated to align with the date of your next Figma invoice, automatically renew, and you’ll be charged the above amount monthly until you cancel in the Admin dashboard.

By clicking “Confirm” you agree to the {figmaAiTermsLink}, including that you will be automatically charged for the Figma AI credits your organization uses quarterly in arrears, at the price per credit and up to the spend limit identified above.

By clicking “Confirm” you agree to the {figmaAiTermsLink}, including that you will be automatically charged for the Figma AI credits your team uses monthly in arrears, at the price per credit and up to the spend limit identified above.

billing.purchase_ai_credits.summary.esti.string: Your costs will be prorated for the remainder of your annual plan and added to your {invoiceDate, date, medium} invoice.

We’ll update your plan credits, and they’ll reset each month until your plan renewal on {invoiceDate, date, medium}.
Your plan credits will update {invoiceDate, date, medium}. Team members can use your previous credit subscription until then.
Your plan credits won't be canceled, and are covered by your previous payment. They'll reset each month until your plan renews on {invoiceDate, date, medium}.
Your costs will be prorated for the remainder of your annual plan and added to your {invoiceDate, date, medium} invoice, along with a refund for your previous credit subscription.
Your costs will be prorated for the remainder of your annual plan and added to your {invoiceDate, date, medium} invoice. Your previous credit subscription will be partially refunded.

Your costs will be prorated for the remainder of your annual plan and added to your {invoiceDate, date, medium} invoice. Your previous credit subscription will be partially refunded on your {originalInvoiceLink}.

Your costs will be prorated for the remainder of your annual plan and added to your {invoiceDate, date, medium} invoice. Your previous credit subscription will be partially refunded and applied to your next invoice.
Your costs will be prorated for the remainder of the month and added to your {invoiceDate, date, medium} invoice, along with a refund for your previous credit subscription.
Your costs will be prorated for the remainder of the month and added to your {invoiceDate, date, medium} invoice. Your previous credit subscription will be partially refunded.

Your costs will be prorated for the remainder of the month and added to your {invoiceDate, date, medium} invoice. Your previous credit subscription will be partially refunded on your {originalInvoiceLink}.

community.skills_page.example_skills.sub.string: Dig into these GitHub repos from the Figma community to see how skills are created. Use them as reference and inspiration as you build your own.

community.skills_page.extended_descripti.string: Figma skills are markdown files that guide AI models on how to work with Figma primitives and concepts. Each skill defines a specific set of actions and context, such as creating components, generating layouts, or syncing variables between code and Figma. This page is designed to help you discover what’s possible. You can explore a curated set of skills, understand how they work, and choose the ones that fit your workflow. Each skill links to its source, where you can view, install, and use it in your development environment. Figma skills make it easier to move faster between code and design, reuse existing systems, and automate repetitive tasks.

community.skills_page.foundational_skill.string: Start by downloading this foundational skill that teaches your AI agents what tokens, variables, styles, and components are and how to use them on the canvas.

community.weave_page.extended_descriptio.string: Weave is Figma’s AI workflow tool for generating images, video, and creative assets using prompts and layered controls. This page showcases curated Weave workflow templates built by the team and Community creators. Explore ready-made workflows, filter by use case, and open them in Weave to customize and create your own variations. Whether you’re experimenting with visual styles, automating production assets, or prototyping ideas, these workflows help you move from concept to output faster.

living_designs.code_layer.suggestions.di.string: Create a digital clock showing the time in San Francisco. Modern, minimalist design with smooth animations when digits change. Include subtle grid lines in the background. Implement a 'frosted glass' backdrop filter effect. Add a micro hover effect. Make sure the text is immediately visible.

living_designs.code_layer.suggestions.gr.string: Make a background component that shows a subtle gradient animation. It should use pastel colors. Make the animation speed configurable via a property.

living_designs.code_layer.suggestions.st.string: Make a component that displays a numeric stat. It should have a single large number (that can be set via a property) and a description underneath (that can also be set via a property). When the component is visible on the viewport, the stat should smoothly count up from 0 to the number. Make sure the text is immediately visible.

living_designs.suggestions.dashboard.pro.string:
Create an intuitive and visually appealing dashboard that provides users with:
  - Sticky top nav bar with logo, links, search field, new project button, avatar dropdown
  - Hero panel with summary, achievement stats and data visualizations
  - Summary cards
  - A table of entries

living_designs.suggestions.gradient_gall.string:
A modern gradient gallery page where tapping a gradient transitions to a lightbox of a card with details about the color
  - Main page shows 8 colors in a 2x4 grid centered on the page, no text or titles or other elements
  - Smoothly animate the layout transition of the card

living_designs.suggestions.signup_flow.p.string:
Design a user-friendly sign-up flow for a website that aims to provide an effortless onboarding experience for new users.
  - Left panel: Visual storytelling with image carousel, tagline, smooth auto-advances
  - Right panel: Signup form, offer alternative signin methods (google and apple)
  - Break out fields into a multi-step flow and show progress between steps

seat_billing_terms.banner.info.main_text.string: You have until {renewalDate, date, long} to accept our updated contract terms.

seat_billing_terms.banner.urgent.descrip.string: Please review and accept these updates, or your account may be locked.
Your account will be suspended on {renewalDate, date, long} unless you review and accept our updated contract terms.

seat_billing_terms.banner.urgent.main_te.string: Accept our updated contract terms to continue using Figma.

seat_billing_terms.modal.agreement_terms.string: By clicking “Accept terms,” you agree to the order terms outlined here and in the {softwareAgreementLink} and confirm that you have authority to act on your organization’s behalf.

seat_billing_terms.modal.confirm_terms.string: To keep using Figma, read and confirm the updated terms about seats and pricing on the following page. If you’ve already signed a contract including these updates, that contract will apply instead.

seat_billing_terms.modal.evolving_terms.string: We’re evolving our seats from single-product to multi-product.

seat_billing_terms.modal.finish_and_agre.string: Finish and agree to keep using Figma

seat_billing_terms.modal.please_review.string: Please review some important updates for admins

seat_billing_terms.modal.renewal_price_u.string: At the contract renewal for {organizationName} on {renewalDate, date, long}, prices for {planTier} seats will update to the following:

seat_billing_terms.modal.success.string: Thanks for accepting the updated terms.

seat_billing_terms.modal.tos.additional_.string: You can still purchase and manage seats any time in your Admin dashboard, but the way fees are calculated will change. New and upgraded seats added during your contract term will be charged from the date the seat is approved, and prorated on a daily basis until the end of your contract term.

seat_billing_terms.modal.tos.invoices.te.string: Your current payment method and due dates will be carried over to your future renewal and invoices. All fees will be invoiced annually in advance, with new, prorated seat additions billed in quarterly invoices when applicable. Quarterly invoices for any seat additions will be sent 3, 6, 9, and 12 months after renewal.

seat_billing_terms.modal.tos.learn_more.string: Learn more about our {updatedSeatsLink}. If you have questions about these terms, reach out to our {supportLink}.

seat_billing_terms.modal.tos.subscriptio.string: At your upcoming renewal, prices will be based on the amount of seats at the renewal date multiplied by seat types’ annualized rates. In all following renewals, your subscription price for renewals will be calculated the same way it has been in the past—based on the number of seats purchased in the previous subscription period (including new seat additions) or the number of seats in {organizationName}'s account on the renewal date—whichever is greater. We’ll take that number of seats and multiply it by the applicable seat types’ prices at their annualized rates to determine your costs.

We’ll transition your {planTier} to new pricing at your next 12-month renewal on {renewalDate, date, long}. Your subscription will continue to renew for additional one-year terms on the then-current {softwareAgreementLink}, unless you give (or Figma provides) written notice of non-renewal at least 30 days before the end of the contract term. These terms will apply to your plan’s future auto-renewals.

workspace.create_confirmation_modal.desc.string: When you first create a workspace, members of your organization will get the following new features right away:
A browsing experience for discovering workspaces
Libraries are organized by workspace in the Libraries modal
A prompt to choose their workspace (once you enable it)
Search results can be filtered by workspace
{learnMore} about these features or reach out to your account manager if you have questions.
